title: OS commands should not be vulnerable to argument injection attacks
description: OS command argument injections occur when applications allow the execution of operating system commands from untrusted data but the untrusted data is limited to the arguments. It is not possible to directly inject arbitrary commands that compromise the underlying operating system, but the behavior of the executed command still might be influenced in a way that allows to expand access, for example, execution of arbitrary commands. The security of the application depends on the behavior of the application that is executed.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
string fileName = Request.Form["file"];
Process.Start("notepad", fileName); // Noncompliant: untrusted input as argument
compliant:
string fileName = Path.GetFileName(Request.Form["file"]); // Compliant: sanitized file name
Process.Start("notepad", fileName);

checks.message: Process.Start call detected - ensure arguments are properly sanitized to prevent injection

logic.detection_conditions: Uses regex to detect Process.Start method calls that could be vulnerable to argument injection
logic.triggering_conditions: CallExpression nodes containing Process.Start calls, excluding those with obvious sanitization
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes test methods, logging, hardcoded strings, and calls with sanitization methods
never_trigger_on: Calls with hardcoded string literals only
logic.reasoning: Detects Process.Start calls which can be vulnerable to argument injection if user input is passed directly as arguments. Excludes cases with obvious sanitization or hardcoded safe values.
